verses.text: Jephthah, son of a harlot, is driven out by his brethren. Outcast. The rejected is raised up.
Jephthah, son of a harlot, is driven out by his brethren. Outcast. The rejected is raised up.
Jephthah, son of a harlot, is driven out by his brethren. Outcast. The rejected is raised up.
When war comes, the elders call him to lead. Called. The despised is now sought.
When war comes, the elders call him to lead. Called. The despised is now sought.
When war comes, the elders call him to lead. Called. The despised is now sought.
He agrees to be their head if God gives victory. Head. He returns in faith.
He agrees to be their head if God gives victory. Head. He returns in faith.
He agrees to be their head if God gives victory. Head. He returns in faith.
He agrees to be their head if God gives victory. Head. He returns in faith.
He agrees to be their head if God gives victory. Head. He returns in faith.
He sends to the Ammonite king about the dispute. Inquiry. He seeks peace first.
He sends to the Ammonite king about the dispute. Inquiry. He seeks peace first.
He recounts Israel's history with the land. History. He pleads their right.
He recounts Israel's history with the land. History. He pleads their right.
He recounts Israel's history with the land. History. He pleads their right.
He recounts Israel's history with the land. History. He pleads their right.
He recounts Israel's history with the land. History. He pleads their right.
God gave them Sihon's land, not Ammon's. Right. The claim is just.
God gave them Sihon's land, not Ammon's. Right. The claim is just.
God gave them Sihon's land, not Ammon's. Right. The claim is just.
God gave them Sihon's land, not Ammon's. Right. The claim is just.
'Wilt not thou possess what Chemosh gives thee?' Argument. He answers their own gods.
'Wilt not thou possess what Chemosh gives thee?' Argument. He answers their own gods.
He appeals to the LORD the Judge to decide. The Judge. God will settle it.
He appeals to the LORD the Judge to decide. The Judge. God will settle it.
He appeals to the LORD the Judge to decide. The Judge. God will settle it.
The king will not hear; Jephthah vows a rash vow. The vow. Zeal outruns wisdom.
The king will not hear; Jephthah vows a rash vow. The vow. Zeal outruns wisdom.
The king will not hear; Jephthah vows a rash vow. The vow. Zeal outruns wisdom.
The king will not hear; Jephthah vows a rash vow. The vow. Zeal outruns wisdom.
God gives him victory over Ammon. Victory. The LORD delivers.
God gives him victory over Ammon. Victory. The LORD delivers.
His daughter meets him; he is grieved by his vow. Grief. The vow brings sorrow.
His daughter meets him; he is grieved by his vow. Grief. The vow brings sorrow.
His daughter meets him; he is grieved by his vow. Grief. The vow brings sorrow.
She bewails her virginity; the custom is kept. Fulfilled. A costly, rash vow.
She bewails her virginity; the custom is kept. Fulfilled. A costly, rash vow.
